required_preferences.fact: Centers deep work around structured, in-person collaboration blocks at the startup’s office during on-site days, reserving independent work for home office days — adapts concentration time based on where she’s working rather than random intervals.

supersedes.fact: Replaces her daily two-hour deep work block with flexible, scattered work intervals throughout the day due to conference keynote prep and travel commitments — now takes early calls and meetings as needed during this period.

required_preferences.fact: Eats out for lunch and adapts to available options during travel or conference preparation periods — only meal-preps lunches on regular weeks at home.

supersedes.fact: Preps lunches for the week every Sunday night, never eats out for lunch on workdays

required_preferences.fact: Temporarily opts for quick meal solutions (like meal kits or prepared foods) during the intensive certification cram period to free up time for study and client frameworks overhaul.
Eats lunch with the startup’s finance team on in-office days — prefers social meals over solo desk lunches while serving as interim CFO.
Coordinates with her daughter's co-parent to share transportation duties for robotics club, preferring to alternate pickups when possible
Commutes by light rail to the tech company’s office, uses this time exclusively for catching up on industry news or financial podcasts — never checks email or answers work calls during commute.

persona: A freelance financial consultant with clients across multiple industries
